justification: All 6 ACs correctly implemented. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED with separate test cases. AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error), returns unknown, and tests verify warn is called exactly once for unknown statuses and never for known ones. AC5 uses floating-point division with toFixed(2) for correct rounding (1099→10.99). AC6 handles both null and undefined via != null guard. 16 tests across 6 describe blocks cover all ACs. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. Logger is injected (makeLogger helper), no global logger. No external dependencies.

observations.text: STATUS_MAP lookup table is clean O(1) design — avoids brittle if/else chains
AC3 explicitly tests both FAILED and DECLINED as separate cases
AC4 tests include edge cases: PROCESSING, empty string, lowercase 'paid', and a negative test confirming no warn for known statuses
AC6 explicitly tests undefined, null, and preservation of provided name
Logger injected via makeLogger() helper — no global dependency
Thoughtful assumption documented about empty-string customer_name not being treated as Anonymous
